title: UI: who's-in dashboard at /
description: Server component reads from whos_in_now view. One card per active employee, grouped by team. Green = in, grey = out, faded = never tapped today. Auto-refresh every 30s via client-side timer that re-fetches. Show count badges (n in, n out).
close_reason: Dashboard at / verified working: auth, query, render, 15 active employees grouped by team, auto-refresh wiring all in place. Confirmed in browser by user.

title: Migration: whos_in_now view (latest tap per employee today, in/out by parity)
description: View joining employees (active=true) ⨝ tap_cards (active=true) ⨝ today's taps from timecard_events_90d. Uses parity rule: odd tap count today = currently IN, even = OUT. Returns employee_id, full_name, team, status (in/out/never), first_tap_today, last_tap_today, tap_count_today. Underlying timezone: Europe/London.

title: Migration: tap_cards table + RLS + partial unique index
description: Create tap_cards (id uuid pk, card_id text, employee_id uuid fk -> employees, active bool default true, deactivated_at timestamptz nullable, notes text, created_at, updated_at). Partial unique index on card_id WHERE active. Enable RLS with read=authenticated, write=authenticated policies. Migration SQL committed to migration/ directory; applied manually via Supabase SQL editor.

title: Epic: Build employee-presence app (who's in + roll-call + hours)
description: Parent epic. Children cover the tap_cards schema, derived SQL views/functions, dashboard pages, JSON API for the e-ink panel, admin UI, and dev seed data. Single hardcoded shift (08:00-17:00 Europe/London) for v1; multi-shift support deferred to a follow-up. RLS enabled on all new tables (read=authenticated, write=authenticated for v1; tightened to admin role in a follow-up).

title: Scaffold app/ from NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md templates
description: Use the file templates in NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md to create app/package.json, app/next.config.ts, app/Dockerfile, app/.dockerignore (mandatory), docker-compose.app.yml, build.sh, .env.example, .gitignore. Substitute <appname>=employee-presence and <NNNN>=<reserved port>.

title: Reserve port and service name in platform-portal/docs/PORTS.md
description: Pick the next free port (3xxx) and service name 'employee-presence'. Edit ../platform-portal/docs/PORTS.md, commit, push. Required before any other scaffolding.

title: Refactor employees_daily_hours to use per-employee shift_pattern
description: Currently the function takes p_shift_start/p_shift_end as parameters with global defaults. With employee_shift_pattern + employee_shift_pattern_day tables in place, the function should LEFT JOIN through to the assigned pattern's per-weekday row to get day_start/day_finish/lunch_start/lunch_finish for each (employee, work_date). Rules: (1) employees with NULL shift_pattern_id continue to use the global default constants; (2) is_working_day for an employee = their pattern has a row for that weekday (or fall back to Mon-Fri default); (3) worked_minutes should subtract unpaid lunch minutes (paid_lunch=false) overlapping the worked time; (4) late_minutes/early_finish_minutes computed against that day's specific day_start/day_finish; (5) function function-arg defaults remain for callers that want a fixed shift override.
close_reason: employees_daily_hours refactored to JOIN through to assigned shift_pattern. NULL pattern → fallback to function-arg defaults (Mon-Fri). Per-weekday day_start/day_finish/lunch_start/lunch_finish from employee_shift_pattern_day. Unpaid lunch overlap deducted per pair. Verified end-to-end: assigned standard 08-17 pattern with 30-min unpaid lunch to Carl, all his Mon-Fri days dropped exactly 30 min, Sat correctly is_working_day=false.

title: Schema: add employees.deactivated_at + temporal filter for /hours
description: Currently employees.active is a boolean — when an employee leaves, they vanish from /hours retroactively too, even though they were active during the queried window. Add deactivated_at timestamptz (set automatically when active flips false, mirroring employee_cards). Update employees_daily_hours to include employees whose active period overlaps the query window: (deactivated_at IS NULL OR deactivated_at >= p_start_date) AND created_at < p_end_date + 1. Dashboard (employees_whos_in_now) keeps the simple active=true filter — only /hours needs the temporal logic. Also add a small UI for setting active=false in the employees admin (when that exists).
close_reason: Implemented as part of /hours debugging: migration 007 added employees.deactivated_at + auto-set trigger, migration 003 updated to use temporal-overlap filter (deactivated_at IS NULL OR deactivated_at >= p_start_date).

title: API: GET /api/blacklisted-cards JSON endpoint for M5 firmware blacklist sync
description: Companion endpoint to the e-ink roll-call API. Returns array of deactivated card_ids (employee_cards.active = false) so M5 readers can refuse them locally with audio/visual feedback (red LED + buzzer) and avoid the 'silent no-op' UX where a tap on a lost card returns nothing. M5 firmware pulls on its own timer. Pairs with this issue: separate firmware-side work to consume the list, store it in flash, and check before publishing the tap event.
close_reason: GET /api/blacklisted-cards live at app/app/api/blacklisted-cards/route.ts. Returns {generated_at, count, card_ids} for M5 firmware to pull on a timer. Verified shape with empty list response.

title: UI: tap_cards admin at /admin/cards
description: List of tap_cards with employee name, status. List of unmapped card_ids seen in recent timecard_events (no row in active tap_cards). Forms to: assign unmapped card to employee, deactivate a card (sets active=false, deactivated_at=now()), edit notes. No add-by-hand card row needed in v1 (cards self-discover from events).
close_reason: Admin UI complete: three sections (Unassigned/Active/Deactivated), assign + deactivate flows, suspicious-tap visibility panel for deactivated card reuse. Migrations 005 (drop updated_at trigger) + 006 (unconditional unique on card_id) applied.

title: UI: hours analysis report at /hours
description: Date range picker (default this week), table of employees x days with hours and anomaly flags. Reads from daily_hours function. Filter by team. Export to CSV button (low-priority follow-up).
close_reason: Hours analysis report at /hours working end-to-end. Date range, team filter, summary tiles, anomaly badges all functional against the legacy seed data (Sept 2024). Resolved RLS gap by switching employees_whos_in_now view + employees_daily_hours function to security definer mode (controlled read surface; raw timecard_events stays service_role-only).

title: API: GET /api/roll-call JSON endpoint for e-ink panel
description: Returns current who's-in payload as JSON: timestamp, employees array with name, team, status, last_tap_at. Stable schema (the panel pulls on its own timer). No auth for v1 (LAN-only) — revisit if exposed publicly.
close_reason: GET /api/roll-call live at app/app/api/roll-call/route.ts. Verified returning 15 active employees with counts. Uses server-only supabaseAdmin (SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY) to bypass RLS — same pattern reusable for x0q blacklist endpoint.

title: UI: roll-call view at /roll-call (web fallback to e-ink)
description: Large-text printable view, sortable by team/name, click-to-mark accounted-for (client-side only, no persistence needed). Print-friendly stylesheet. Used as fallback when the e-ink panel is unavailable.

title: Lib: shift constants (08:00-17:00 Europe/London) + early/late helpers
description: app/lib/shift.ts with WORKING_DAYS, SHIFT_START, SHIFT_END, TIMEZONE constants and helpers like isLate(ts), isEarly(ts), expectedShiftFor(date). Designed so a future shift_patterns table can replace the constant lookup with a per-employee query.
close_reason: Shift constants and helpers committed at app/lib/shift.ts. Constants intentionally duplicated in upcoming daily_hours SQL function — both layers will switch to shift_patterns lookups together when per-employee schedules arrive.

title: Migration: daily_hours SQL function (pair taps, hours, anomaly flags)
description: Function or view that, for a given date range, returns per-employee per-day rows with: total clocked hours (paired tap deltas), first_in, last_out, missed_clock_in flag, missed_clock_out flag (odd tap count), late_arrival vs shift_start, early_finish vs shift_end. Underlying basis for the /hours report.

title: First deploy on the Pi
description: ssh pi@10.0.0.75; create /opt/pss-employee-presence; git clone; docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d. Test at http://10.0.0.75:3000/employee-presence/ (via gateway).

title: First build + push to ghcr.io
description: chmod +x build.sh && ./build.sh — builds ARM64 image and pushes to ghcr.io/ukstevem/pss-employee-presence with both :<sha> and :latest tags. Requires ../platform-portal/.env to exist (sibling layout).

title: Create GitHub repo ukstevem/pss-employee-presence and push initial commit
description: Create a public/private repo at https://github.com/new (do NOT init with README/license/.gitignore). Then: git remote add origin <url>; git add .; git commit -m 'initial scaffold'; git push -u origin main.
close_reason: Repo created at https://github.com/ukstevem/pss-employee-presence; initial scaffold pushed as commit 6e7300a

title: Copy and freeze shared packages (ui, auth, supabase)
description: cp -r ../platform-portal/packages/{ui,auth,supabase} app/packages/. Then edit each app/packages/*/package.json to remove workspace:* deps (keep peerDependencies and real npm deps only). Inter-package imports resolve via the app's own node_modules root.

title: Wire route into platform-portal nginx production.conf
description: Add location /employee-presence/ block to platform-portal/docker/nginx/production.conf pointing at http://employee-presence:<NNNN>. Rebuild and push the gateway image. See NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md 'Wiring into the gateway'.

title: UI: shift pattern admin (CRUD parent + per-day rows + assign to employees)
description: Admin page for managing employee_shift_pattern + employee_shift_pattern_day. List existing patterns with their day rows. Create/edit a pattern: name, description, paid_lunch, active. Edit per-weekday rows inline (Mon-Sun grid, each can be filled in or left blank for off). Assign-to-employee dropdown on the employees admin (not yet built). Until this UI exists, manage via SQL editor.
close_reason: Shift pattern admin live at /admin/shifts/. Pattern CRUD with 7-day grid editor (toggle days on/off, day_start/day_finish/lunch per row). Employee assignment table with per-row dropdown. Verified by user: schedules assigned and a new 'office' team member added.

title: Schema: add employees.hire_date (or joined_at) for proper tenure filtering
description: Currently the /hours temporal filter only uses deactivated_at, so every still-active and recently-departed employee appears in *every* past date range — including periods before they were actually hired. employees.created_at can't be used as a proxy because it represents when the row was inserted into our system (March 2026 for migrated data), not when the person joined the company. Add hire_date date (nullable for migrated rows where unknown) and update employees_daily_hours to: WHERE (hire_date IS NULL OR hire_date <= p_end_date) AND (deactivated_at IS NULL OR deactivated_at >= p_start_date).
close_reason: Implemented as migration 008: added employees.hire_date date column, backfilled from created_at (which you'd manually corrected to be hire dates). Function 003 now filters on hire_date instead of created_at. NULL hire_date is treated as 'always' so newly-added rows without it set still appear in reports.

title: Dev: seed script for realistic test taps
description: Script that inserts ~5 active employees + tap_cards mappings + a week of plausible tap events (mostly clean in/out pairs, some missed clock-outs, some late arrivals). Lets us develop the UI before live readers are pumping events. Idempotent.

value: pss-employee-presence DB naming convention: tables and views use the employees_* prefix to group with the employees roster table in Supabase's table editor. Examples: employee_cards (singular table), employees_whos_in_now (view), employees_daily_hours (function). The plural/singular inconsistency is acceptable; alphabetical grouping is the goal. Other card use cases (door access, tool checkout) get their own tables — don't generalise employee_cards.

pss-employee-presence reserved port 3011, service name 'employee-presence', basePath '/employee-presence' on 2026-04-25. PORTS.md updated, app scaffolded from NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md templates, packages copied and frozen (workspace:* removed from @platform/auth).

Bootstrapped pss-employee-presence on 2026-04-25 via platform-portal/scripts/new-app.sh. App name: employee-presence. Convention: port reservation in platform-portal/docs/PORTS.md is done before any other work.
